pi_items.metadata_value: 0-4 days = too fresh (excess CO2, inconsistent extraction)
pi_items.verified_value: 0-4 days: Too fresh - CO2 gas prevents proper water contact with grounds, causing extraction inconsistencies and thin, sharp flavor
pi_items.source: Web search: Multiple specialty coffee sources (Fellow, Achilles Coffee Roasters, Clive Coffee) confirm CO2 released in first 24-72 hours causes channeling and uneven extraction

pi_items.verified_value: 5-14 days: Peak flavor window - World Barista Championship competitors routinely use coffee in this 5-14 day range; professional standard for optimal extraction and flavor
pi_items.source: Web search: Multiple sources including specialty coffee roasters and World Barista Championship standards. General Warfield's Coffee: 7-10 day window. Perfect Daily Grind: 4-10 days. Roast Aroma: 5-14 days matches WBC competition practices

pi_items.metadata_value: 15-21 days = still good but flavor declining
pi_items.verified_value: 15-21 days: Still drinkable but flavor degradation beginning - Sources indicate optimal window extends to 21 days for filter coffee, after which volatile aromatics start declining more rapidly
pi_items.source: Web search: General Warfield's Coffee and Monastery Coffee confirm 7-21 day optimal window for filter coffee. Flavor deterioration accelerates after 21 days

pi_items.verified_value: 22+ days: Stale - Flavor degradation accelerates significantly after 21-22 days. Best to consume within 2-4 weeks (14-28 days). After 3 weeks, flavor and aroma loss becomes noticeable
pi_items.source: Web search: America's Test Kitchen, Driftaway Coffee, and multiple roasters confirm flavor deterioration around 3 weeks (21 days). After this point, volatile compounds break down rapidly

pi_items.metadata_value: =TODAY()-C2 where C2 is roast date column
pi_items.verified_value: Formula structure is correct: =TODAY()-C2 calculates days elapsed since roast date in column C. This is standard spreadsheet date arithmetic
pi_items.source: Code analysis: setup_task.sh places roast dates in column C (position 2), task.json and README.md specify formula =TODAY()-C2, which mathematically computes the difference in days

pi_items.metadata_value: Data spans last 25 days (roasts from 25 days ago to today)
pi_items.verified_value: Data spans last 25 days - setup_task.sh creates roasts with dates ranging from DATE_25_DAYS_AGO through TODAY, providing realistic distribution across freshness windows
pi_items.source: Code analysis: setup_task.sh lines 14-26 define dates from 25 days ago to today, with 12 coffee entries distributed across this range

pi_items.metadata_value: Column C = Roast Date, Column F = Days Since Roast
pi_items.verified_value: Column structure confirmed: Setup_task.sh creates CSV with Roast Date in column 3 (C) and instructs agent to add Days Since Roast in column F
pi_items.source: Code analysis: setup_task.sh line 30 shows CSV header order; lines 82-86 position cursor at column F

pi_items.metadata_value: Ethiopia Yirgacheffe is a sample coffee origin in the data
pi_items.verified_value: Ethiopia Yirgacheffe is a real coffee origin from Yirgacheffe region in southern Ethiopia, known for bright acidity and floral/fruity notes
pi_items.source: Web search: Verified as micro-region in Sidamo, Ethiopia; widely considered birthplace of coffee. Multiple specialty roasters confirm authenticity

pi_items.metadata_value: Colombia Huila is a sample coffee origin in the data
pi_items.verified_value: Colombia Huila is a real coffee region in southwestern Colombia, the country's largest coffee producing region, known for fruit/caramel notes and bright acidity
pi_items.source: Web search: Verified as major Colombian coffee region with Denomination of Origin status. Located in Colombian Massif near Nevado de Huila volcano

pi_items.metadata_value: Kenya AA is a sample coffee origin/grade in the data
pi_items.verified_value: Kenya AA is a real Kenyan coffee grade - AA designates largest bean size (7.2-7.8mm), highest quality grade from Kenya's high-altitude regions
pi_items.source: Web search: Verified Kenya grading system; AA is the premium grade. Kenya AA known for complex flavor profile with berry, citrus, and floral notes

pi_items.metadata_value: Data is generated dynamically with relative dates for realistic freshness distribution
pi_items.verified_value: Data is synthetic - setup_task.sh generates coffee_roast_log.csv dynamically using bash date commands with relative dates (e.g., 'date -d "25 days ago"')
pi_items.source: Code analysis: setup_task.sh lines 14-42 show dynamic CSV generation with bash date calculations; file created fresh during task setup

privileged_info_summary: The task involves managing a coffee roast freshness tracker with dynamically generated synthetic data spanning the last 25 days. All claimed coffee freshness windows are verified as accurate: 0-4 days (too fresh due to CO2 interference), 5-14 days (peak flavor, matching World Barista Championship standards), 15-21 days (drinkable but declining), and 22+ days (stale). The formula structure (=TODAY()-C2) is correct for calculating days since roast with dates in column C. Sample coffee origins (Ethiopia Yirgacheffe, Colombia Huila, Kenya AA) are all real, well-documented coffee regions/grades used by specialty roasters. The dataset contains 12 coffee entries with realistic roast level designations (Light, Medium, Medium-Light, Dark) distributed across the 25-day freshness spectrum.
